words.example: There has been a significant improvement in public transport this year.

words.definition: to give time, money, or resources for a purpose
words.example: Governments must allocate more funding to teacher training.

words.definition: to improve the quality or value of something
words.example: Good feedback can enhance students' academic writing.

words.definition: able to be trusted or depended on
words.example: Researchers need reliable data before drawing conclusions.

words.definition: the result of an action or process
words.example: The outcome of education reform depends on consistent implementation.

words.definition: to continue something at the same level or condition
words.example: Students must maintain focus during the reading test.
Time is a serious constraint for many working learners.

words.definition: a method of dealing with something
words.example: A balanced approach to revision is more effective than panic studying.
